wine_museums.name: WiMu Museo del Vino a Barolo
wine_museums.description: The WiMu wine museum inside the Castello Falletti di Barolo, opened 2010 and designed by Francois Confino. Twenty-five rooms over five floors trace wine in culture, history and the senses above the Barolo cellars.
wine_museums.admission: Full ticket around EUR 9; combined ticket with the Enoteca Regionale del Barolo available

wine_museums.name: Casa Martini Museum
wine_museums.address: Piazza Luigi Rossi 2, Frazione Pessione, 10023 Chieri TO, Italy
wine_museums.description: The museum galleries at Casa Martini in Pessione tell the story of Martini & Rossi vermouth since 1864, including a museum of the history of oenology in the cellars beneath the historic plant.
wine_museums.admission: Guided tour with tasting from around EUR 25; museum access included in the visit

verified.address_quoted: Piazza Luigi Rossi, 2 - Frazione Pessione - 10023, Chieri

wine_museums.name: Museo Carpano
wine_museums.description: The Carpano vermouth museum on the first floor of Eataly Torino Lingotto, in the brand's former factory. It traces vermouth from Benedetto Carpano's 1786 Turin recipe through the Punt e Mes and Antica Formula era.
wine_museums.admission: Museum visit free; guided tours and tastings by reservation

wine_museums.name: Castello di Grinzane Cavour and Langhe Museum
wine_museums.address: Via Castello 5, 12060 Grinzane Cavour CN, Italy
wine_museums.description: The 11th-century castle above the Langhe vineyards houses the Langhe ethnographic museum, Cavour family heirlooms and the Enoteca Regionale Piemontese Cavour, the first regional wine shop in Piedmont, set up 1967.
wine_museums.admission: Castle and museum ticket around EUR 7; the regional enoteca can be visited free of charge
